title: Better trivy command for ROOTFS SBOM generation
body:
### Issue Description

Currently the default command for scanning the rootfs is `--sbom-scanner-command="trivy filesystem -q {ROOTFS} --format cyclonedx --output {OUTPUT}"`

However the [trivy docs](https://trivy.dev/latest/docs/target/rootfs/) indicate a `rootfs` target would be more appropriate, and give an [example](https://trivy.dev/latest/docs/advanced/container/embed-in-dockerfile/) of scanning a container root filesystem. `rootfs` does enable scanning of [more language files](https://trivy.dev/latest/docs/coverage/language/#supported-languages) than `filesystem` although they should be picked up by the CONTEXT scan?
